title: The Battle for Sullivan’s Island
summary: Marshy, sandy terrain and an impassable inlet helped colonial forces repel British forces during a pivotal battle on the barrier island near Charleston, South Carolina, on June 28, 1776.

title: Rocket Lab wins NASA award for three Electron launches
summary:
NASA has selected Rocket Lab to launch a pair of science missions on three Electron rockets in 2027.

title: Expedition 73 Crew Reflects on Science, Teamwork, and Life in Orbit
summary: On June 16, astronauts and cosmonauts gathered at Space Center Houston to share stories from their missions aboard the International Space Station and recognize the teamwork and people on the ground that made their missions possible.  The Expedition 73 Welcome Home Ceremony brought together members of NASA’s SpaceX Crew-10, Soyuz MS-27, and NASA’s SpaceX Crew-11 missions. title: NASA, US Small Business Administration to Announce Partnership
summary: NASA and the U.S. Small Business Administration will sign a memorandum of agreement during a ceremony at 1 p.m. EDT, Monday, June 29, at NASA Headquarters in Washington. The agreement will create a new interagency initiative that directly responds to President Donald J. Trump’s National Space Policy and supports the growth of the American space […]

title: NASA Welcomes Botswana as 68th Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: The Republic of Botswana signed the Artemis Accords Thursday during a ceremony hosted by NASA at the agency’s headquarters in Washington, becoming the sixth African nation to join a growing community of nations committed to the peaceful, transparent, and responsible exploration of space. “It is my privilege to welcome Botswana as the newest signatory of the Artemis Accords,” said NASA Deputy […]

title: Botswana signs the Artemis Accords
summary:
The African nation of Botswana is the latest country to sign the Artemis Accords outlining best practices for space exploration.

title: NASA Selects Rocket Lab to Launch Sun, Earth Science Missions
summary: NASA has selected Rocket Lab to provide the launch service for both the agency’s PolSIR (Polarized Submillimeter Ice-cloud Radiometer) and Total and Spectral Solar Irradiance Sensor-2 (TSIS-2) missions. The two selections are part of NASA’s Venture-Class Acquisition of Dedicated and Rideshare (VADR) launch services contract. This contract allows the agency to award fixed-price indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity launch […]
